title: Security CI/CD Pipeline — PR Gate (red + blue, MITRE-aligned)
description: An AppSec / DevSecOps pull-request gate, built as a faithful lift of skills from mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills (Apache-2.0). The 'Anthropic' in that repo name is the maintainer's naming, not an official Anthropic repo. Each node lifts a specific source SKILL.md (cited in its STEP.md): the on-PR scan fan-out and security gate from implementing-devsecops-security-scanning; the six scans from implementing-semgrep-for-custom-sast-rules (SAST), performing-sca-dependency-scanning-with-snyk (SCA), implementing-secret-scanning-with-gitleaks (secrets), exploiting-sql-injection-vulnerabilities (variant/taint), performing-cryptographic-audit-of-application (crypto/timing), implementing-fuzz-testing-in-cicd-with-aflplusplus (mutation); triage from prioritizing-vulnerabilities-with-cvss-scoring + performing-cve-prioritization-with-kev-catalog + implementing-epss-score-for-vulnerability-prioritization; red-team from executing-red-team-exercise + performing-threat-emulation-with-atomic-red-team; blue-team from building-detection-rules-with-sigma + performing-yara-rule-development-for-detection; threat intel from analyzing-apt-group-with-mitre-navigator. The knowledge is in the arrows: six scans run in parallel, then triage dedups before red-team — running PoC validation on un-deduped findings wastes ~90% of cycles on false positives — so red-team emulation goes AFTER triage; blue-team synthesizes detections from the same triaged set in parallel with red-team; and the severity gate decides block/allow from the COMBINED red+blue result (confirmed-exploitable + detection coverage), never from any single tool's severity.

intake.name: Intake PR
intake.does: Parse the pull-request diff and the dependency manifest into a scan target: changed files, the new endpoint/handler, the dependency bump, languages, and the commit range. This is the on-PR trigger that fans out to the six scanners.

threat_intel.name: Threat Intel Feed
threat_intel.does: Pull the relevant ATT&CK technique set and advisory/KEV/EPSS feed for this stack (payment webhook handler): which techniques an adversary would chain against these weakness classes, and which advisories touch the bumped dependency.

scan_sast.name: SAST (Semgrep)
scan_sast.does: Run static analysis with security-audit + owasp-top-ten + custom org rules over the changed code. Emit SARIF-style findings with CWE/OWASP metadata and ERROR-severity gating candidates.

scan_sca.name: SCA (Snyk / dependency scan)
scan_sca.does: Software Composition Analysis over the manifest + lockfile. Resolve direct vs transitive, match against the vuln database, record CVE, CVSS, exploit maturity, reachability, and whether a fix exists.

scan_secrets.name: Secret Scan (Gitleaks)
scan_secrets.does: Scan the PR commit range and diff for hardcoded credentials, keys, and tokens using default + custom org rules. Record rule, file:line, entropy, and whether the finding is new vs baseline.

scan_variant.name: Variant Analysis (taint)
scan_variant.does: Taint-track the user-controlled sources flagged by SAST to every reachable sink to find sibling injection variants the single-pattern rule missed (e.g. the same tainted value flowing into a second query / a log sink).

scan_crypto_timing.name: Crypto / Timing Audit
scan_crypto_timing.does: Audit the cryptographic primitives the new handler uses: hashing/signature algorithms, cipher modes, key handling, and — critically — whether the HMAC/signature comparison is constant-time. Flags timing-unsafe compares and weak algorithms with CWE + remediation.

scan_mutation.name: Mutation / Fuzz
scan_mutation.does: Coverage-guided fuzzing of the new parser (the signature-header decoder) under ASan, seeded from valid inputs. Record crashes, the crashing input class, and the memory-safety CWE.

triage.name: Triage (dedup + CVSS/EPSS/KEV)
triage.does: Fan in all six scans. Deduplicate findings that share a root cause (the SAST SQLi and the taint variant are one tainted source), then score each surviving finding with CVSS v4.0, enrich with EPSS + CISA KEV, and assign a P-level + remediation SLA. This is what makes red-team cheap: PoC validation runs on the deduped set, not raw scanner output.

red_team.name: Red Team (ATT&CK emulation)
red_team.does: Run authorized adversary emulation / PoC validation against the DEDUPED findings only, chaining them into an attack path and mapping each step to an ATT&CK technique. Marks each finding confirmed-exploitable or not-reproducible, with the chained business impact.

blue_team.name: Blue Team (Sigma + YARA)
blue_team.does: Synthesize detection-as-code from the same triaged findings (purple-team output): Sigma rules for the exploitation behavior and a YARA rule for the secret/artifact, each mapped to its ATT&CK technique, so the gap the scan found also gets monitored.

severity_gate.name: Severity Gate
severity_gate.does: Compute the PR block/allow decision from the COMBINED red+blue result: any finding that is both High+ (triage CVSS) AND confirmed-exploitable (red-team) blocks; detection coverage (blue-team) is recorded as a compensating control but does not by itself unblock. Emits the gate decision.

pr_comment.name: PR Comment
pr_comment.does: Render the gate decision as the comment posted back on the pull request: the verdict, the blocking findings with fix guidance, and the deployed detections — what a developer sees on the PR.

posture_report.name: Posture Report
posture_report.does: Compose the long-running security-posture artifact: the full finding inventory, the red-team attack narrative + ATT&CK coverage, the blue-team detection coverage, MTTD/SLA posture, and the trend tie-back. The durable record behind the gate.

deliverable.description: A PR-gate verdict for the pull request under review: the security posture report (posture_report.md) and the PR comment (pr_comment.md) developers act on, backed by the triaged + scored finding set and the severity-gate's allow/block decision. The gate decision is derived from the combined red-team (confirmed-exploitable) and blue-team (detection coverage) result, not from any single scanner's severity.
